title: CLI lacks a --show-schema contract preview before block writes
description: file-block-item.ts prints the block contract (array_key + required fields + field types + id-pattern) before a write so the author sees what is expected; the CLI has no one-shot preview. This is the same contract-blindness that, with FGAP-020, drove the evidence-must-be-array failure while filing FGAP-019.

evidence.reference: script prints the block contract (array_key/required/types/id-pattern) before write
GAP-4 in the parity survey, categorized CLI-only-shared-helper

impact: A CLI user filing into an unfamiliar block cannot preview the contract from the CLI, raising write-time validation failures (the failure mode that surfaced FGAP-020).
proposed_resolution: Add a --show-schema global in parseOpArgs; on a block-mutation op, call the lifted loadBlockSchema/resolveBlockItemSchema path, print the preview, return 0, in an early branch in main mirroring the parsed.help branch (cli.ts:464-467). Op frozen. Re-confirm via the canonical pipeline.
